🟢 Under control

🆕 New since last digest

  • INC-14159 — P2 ElastiCache memory alert, lead acked in 10s, Monitoring within 8 min - clean so far
  • INC-14151 — P2 MeridianBet urgent bonus-validation bug, fix merged+deployed+partner confirmed within ~1h16m
  • INC-14158 — P3 QBetCRM slow processing, ~20 min old, lead assigned
  • INC-14153 — P3 leonbets slow processing, already Monitoring within the hour
  • INC-14152 — P3 SBTSolutions IC ticket (2nd for this partner), lead assigned, too early to assess

✅ Resolved since last digest

  • INC-13853 — closed - Rhino P1, Intercom ticket closed with CSAT; ⚠ incident.io status never synced to match (D15)
  • INC-14082 — clean resolve - EUC RDS T4G downgrade reverted, all 11 alerts cleared
  • INC-14069 — clean - DMS heartbeat alerts, Campeon-Omega decommission confirmed, no partner impact
  • INC-12909 — ⚠ closed - Galabet bonus dispute; incident.io only ever got 1 update despite real Intercom resolution work (D15)
  • INC-14095 — ⚠ closed - betathome RabbitMQ backlog cleared in ~3 min but record sat unclosed ~2.5h (D15, minor)
  • INC-14077 — ⚠ closed - sisutech ping-pong latency auto-resolved but record unclosed ~3h45m (D15, minor)
  • INC-14112 — ⚠ closed - 2nd ElastiCache CPU flare of the day, resolved after instance-upgrade consideration; A4 recurrence pattern worth a look if it happens again
  • INC-14108 — clean resolve - Campeon conversion-service backlog, pods scaled 5->15 in 19 min

🚨 Escalate now 5

INC-8064 slack intercomP1open
[P1] Intercom Incident : H2Bet Production

B5/A4: 50 days unresolved with confirmed ~300/week duplicate bonus leakage; BetConstruct escalation has zero traction and Rewards' own Urgent ticket remains parked until mid-Aug with no movement this cycle.

H2Bet duplicate bonus grants confirmed root cause quickly, but 50 days later still ~300 duplicate grants/week with no fix deployed. BetConstruct account-manager gap means zero external traction since 5 Jun. Rewards team's own Urgent ticket (REW-176) blocked by capacity until mid-Aug. No new activity found this cycle — situation unchanged, financial leakage continuing.

Open breaches
  • A2/A4: no decisive fix after 50 days, Urgent ticket parked a month+
  • A3: minimizing language contradicts confirmed weekly leakage
  • B5: BetConstruct escalation zero traction
  • C10/D15: Intercom closed twice implying resolution while incident.io still open P1
Proposed / pending
  • 503-as-success workaround - rejected
  • PR #122 idempotency fix - closed unmerged
  • PR #123 observability only - merged, no fix
  • REW-176 interim workaround - Urgent, blocked until mid-Aug
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-05-26T14:40
  • temp solution being arranged - blocked until mid-Aug
  • no ETA ever committed for root fix
Timeline (7)
  • 26 May: root cause ID'd same day
  • 05 Jun: BetConstruct outreach dead-ends
  • 11 Jun: confirmed ~300/week duplicates ongoing
  • 06 Jul: Rewards says no capacity until mid-Aug; Intercom closed by Andrea Azzopardi
  • 09 Jul: REW-176 created Urgent, blocked by capacity
  • 15 Jul: no change; no new activity anywhere
  • 15 Jul (this check): no change; no new Slack or Intercom activity

→ Escalate BetConstruct outreach to a named leadership owner today, and push Rewards to scope a stop-gap sooner than mid-Aug given confirmed ongoing weekly financial leakage.

INC-12998 slackP2open
frank-casino: deployment ping-pong is stuck on CrashLoopBackOff

B7/D15/D13: a critical-path crash-loop has been open 5.9+ days with a blank incident.io summary, zero diagnostic content anywhere, and the assigned lead has not engaged in-channel while it remains unresolved.

frank-casino deployment stuck in CrashLoopBackOff, unchanged since last check. incident.io status still 'Investigating', summary still blank, 0 new Slack messages. Lead (Giancarlo Laferla) still assigned but has not posted; incident now 5.9+ days old with no visible progress.

Open breaches
  • D15: blank incident.io summary
  • B7: no visible prioritized action on a critical-path crash-loop now over 5.9 days
  • B5: ~20h escalation-to-accept gap, undocumented lead handoff
  • D13: zero runbook/basic-check evidence anywhere
Timeline (5)
  • 09 Jul: alert fires, lead auto-paged, leaves channel same evening
  • 10 Jul: lead reassigned to Giancarlo ~20h after report
  • 12 Jul: bot nudge unanswered
  • 14 Jul: current lead leaves channel, incident still open
  • 15 Jul 08:40: re-checked - 0 new messages, no updates, summary still blank, status unchanged

→ Escalate immediately to an engineering lead who will actively work the incident in-channel; require a documented root-cause check and a populated incident.io summary without further delay.

INC-12436 slack intercomP2open
[P3] Intercom Incident : Tuohi Production

C9/B5/A4: partner-visible recurrence reported 14 Jul 09:29 remains unanswered ~23h later; partner separately signaled proceeding with termination over this issue with no trace in incident.io; the real fix is only 'prepared'.

223+ Tuohi/Noppa players missed welcome offers due to a lifecycle-entry race condition. Partner told FT internally they were 'proceeding with their termination' over this - never reflected in incident.io. Fix (Kafka switch) was explicitly 'step 1' only; the 14 Jul partner recurrence report remains unanswered - now ~23h with no reply.

Open breaches
  • B5: 2-day lag between termination signal and severity escalation
  • A4: defect recurring 9 days in, after only deployed fix
  • C9/C10: 14 Jul recurrence report unanswered for ~23h and counting
  • D15: incident.io materially rosier than Slack/Intercom reality
Proposed / pending
  • alternative LC trigger - offered
  • PR #58 crash-loop fix - opened
  • synchronous deposit-listener arming (real fix) - only 'prepared', not confirmed deployed
  • Kafka integration switch - deployed but insufficient alone
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-02T18:12
  • last partner message unanswered
  • alternative LC trigger offered
  • verbal commitment to cover in 14 Jul call - not confirmed happened
Timeline (6)
  • 06 Jul: INC-12436 created P3
  • 07 Jul: true scope found, 223 users
  • 08 Jul: partner signals termination internally, severity raised P3->P2 two days later
  • 13 Jul: Kafka switch deployed, 'step 1' only
  • 14 Jul 09:29 UTC: new missing/delayed offers reported by partner, unanswered
  • 15 Jul 08:40: re-checked - still unanswered (~23h), incident.io status unchanged ('Fixing')

→ Escalate to leadership given the explicit partner termination risk; respond to the 14 Jul recurrence immediately; confirm whether the synchronous deposit-listener fix is actually deployed.

INC-13738 slackP2open
Betiro - No unsubs since April 2026

D12/A4 still unresolved: the monitoring gap has now been closed (PR #196 merged after Andrea's direct push), but there is still no legal/compliance escalation for a ~3-month undetected opt-out failure across gambling brands, and 3 newly-flagged brands remain unremediated while other threads close out.

IP-whitelisting change from ~6 Apr silently blocked unsubscribe requests for Betiro, Rivalo, Leonbets since 7 Apr - undetected ~3 months. Fix merged 13 Jul. After Andrea Azzopardi's direct 15 Jul push for the zero-unsub-event monitor, Simon Brown's PR #196 ('daily unsubscribe events count per channel') was merged 16:28 CEST — the monitor Andrea specifically asked for is now landed. Still open: no evidence of legal/compliance escalation for the ~3-month opt-out failure, and 3 newly-flagged brands (Rocketship, Fairspin, Frank Casino) remain unaddressed while Betiro/Rivalo threads are being wound down.

Open breaches
  • D12: still no evidence of legal/compliance escalation for a multi-month opt-out failure across 3 gambling brands
  • C10: no confirmed retroactive remediation of missed unsubscribes
  • partner threads for 2/3 known brands being wound down while 3 newly-flagged brands (Rocketship, Fairspin, Frank Casino) remain unaddressed
Proposed / pending
  • exempt unsubscribe endpoint from IP whitelist - done, merged 13 Jul
  • zero-unsub-event monitoring - delivered 15 Jul 16:28 CEST (PR #196) after direct management push
Timeline (7)
  • 06 Apr: IP whitelisting change introduced without forwarding-header exemption
  • 13 Jul: incident created, fix merged (PR #91)
  • 14 Jul: status -> Monitoring; zero-unsub-event monitor proposed, mid-design
  • 15 Jul 09:45 CEST: Giancarlo reports Betiro/Rivalo 'considered closed', Leonbets pending - no mention of 3 additional flagged brands or legal escalation
  • 15 Jul 10:00 CEST: Andrea Azzopardi pushes Giancarlo/Simon Brown directly to get monitoring live; Simon: 'working on it' - still not live
  • 15 Jul 11:45-11:52 CEST: Giancarlo and Curt Healey discuss a proper long-term alerting fix (better exposer-api status-code handling instead of silently returning 200 OK) - discussion only, no ticket opened yet; bot checked in on the incident's duration
  • 16:28 CEST: PR #196 (daily unsubscribe events count monitor) merged — the monitor Andrea pushed for is now live

→ Escalate to legal/compliance for the multi-month opt-out failure before treating partner threads as closed; open remediation for Rocketship, Fairspin, and Frank Casino.

INC-5630 slackP3open
Coinbar SMS Issues with Credit

A4/D12: 2.5+ months of pure automated alert churn with zero human decision-making since 29 May; incident.io and Slack both confirm the only activity since is another auto-merged Hizlicasino alert (15 Jul) - no human has posted in 47 days.

SMS distributor service has not processed queued messages for all Coinbar brands since before 30 Apr - over 1M messages backlogged. Root cause (external SMS provider outage) identified immediately, but 2.5+ months later there is zero forward progress. Confirmed nothing has moved since 29 May besides more alerts auto-merging in: a further Hizlicasino alert merged in at 15 Jul 10:24 CEST (no consumers, 1376 messages), all machine-generated with no human commentary.

Open breaches
  • A4: chronic multi-month issue never escalated to a decisive fix or vendor-management track - now 47 days of pure automated churn
  • D12/D15: the one substantive question raised (29 May) never answered or tracked - Lead rotated to at least a 6th person, new alerts keep merging in unattended
Proposed / pending
  • Escalate to SMS provider - done repeatedly, no concrete response ever
  • Gustav Tjäder asked to advise next course of action (29 May) - never answered
Timeline (7)
  • 30 Apr: incident created, 1M+ message backlog
  • 25-29 May: Andrea Azzopardi repeatedly asks for an explanation, no answer for 4 days
  • 29 May: lead explains 'not much to do from SOC', pulls in Gustav Tjäder - never answered
  • 14 Jul: Lead reassigned again to James Hodgson, no substantive update since 29 May
  • 15 Jul 10:24 CEST: another Hizlicasino alert (1376 messages, no consumers) auto-merged in - confirms zero human progress
  • 15 Jul 10:24-11:24 CEST: another Hizlicasino alert (1654 msgs, no consumers) merged in; 50+ alerts auto-resolved twice more - still zero human commentary since 29 May
  • 15 Jul 12:46 CEST: another automated merge (INC-14127, Hizlicasino) — 48th+ alert auto-resolved with zero human commentary since 29 May

→ Force a decision: escalate to account/vendor management for a binding SMS-provider ETA or a migration decision, and assign a named owner who must respond rather than letting the lead rotate unattended.

🔴 Needs attention 7

INC-12022 slack intercomP1open
Shortlinks Redirecting to "Forbidden" page

C10: today's promised RCA report still hasn't been delivered — only a clarifying question to the partner — making this a 3rd broken/slipping promise unless the report goes out today.

Cloudflare-origin security check blocked legitimate shortlink/unsubscribe traffic on 2 Jul; mitigated within ~2h, root-cause fix merged same evening. Partner (Estelarbet/PLIN) has been waiting 12+ days for a promised RCA report, with two broken 'update tomorrow' promises. Today (15 Jul) was the latest promised date; Giancarlo sent the partner a clarifying question at 13:17 UTC (asking for the reporting user/email to re-run impact analysis) but the actual RCA report itself has still not been delivered.

Open breaches
  • C10: partner RCA report 12+ days overdue, 2 broken promises; today's promised date has a clarifying reply but not the report itself
  • D15: incident.io status timestamps misrepresent when fix landed
  • A4: same CF/unsub-blocking pattern recurring across 3 incidents (also see INC-13738)
Proposed / pending
  • CF-origin carve-out - merged 02 Jul
  • file-serving revert - merged 02 Jul
  • deliver full partner report - still pending
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-02T13:29
  • last partner message unanswered
  • RCA/report promised repeatedly, still not delivered; promised for 15 Jul (today), not yet sent
Timeline (6)
  • 02 Jul: fixes merged same day
  • 06 Jul: status moved Investigating->Fixing 4 days late
  • 10 Jul: status -> Monitoring (unchanged since)
  • 13-14 Jul: two more broken 'update tomorrow' promises, next promised 15 Jul
  • 15 Jul: no new activity found; promised date arrived with nothing sent yet
  • 13:17 UTC: Giancarlo replies to partner with a clarifying question (re-running impact analysis) — not the promised report itself

→ Send the overdue RCA report to Estelarbet/PLIN today, or proactively set one final concrete date — a 3rd broken promise would be a serious trust issue.

INC-13893 slack intercomP2open
[P2] Intercom Incident : Tuohi Production

C10/C11: partner was given a fix explanation engineers privately concluded doesn't work, and the correction has still not been sent over 24h later; silence in the channel continues.

Tuohi LC39 players missed bonuses, a likely regression of INC-12436's race-condition bug class. Root cause found and code-verified, but the scaling 'fix' given to the partner was later found by engineers not to actually work, and that correction was never sent back. No new activity since last read - silence continues, over 24h since the last partner message.

Open breaches
  • B5: 11h+ silent while 'Investigating' now extending past 24h, zero incident.io updates
  • A4: likely recurrence of INC-12436's bug class, not reflected in the incident record
  • C10/C11: partner given an explanation later found incorrect, never corrected; 3 newly-reported players unaccounted for
Proposed / pending
  • enforce add-before-deposit event ordering - proposed, unimplemented
  • scale scheduled-action-manager replicas - implemented, judged ineffective
  • PR #103 (Redis engine upgrade) - open, review disputed
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-12T10:51
  • last partner message unanswered
  • 9-player ID list delivered
  • scaling explanation given, later found not to fix the issue, correction never sent
Timeline (7)
  • 12 Jul: partner first reports via Intercom
  • 14 Jul 08:17-08:20: possible regression flagged, incident created
  • 14 Jul 12:35: root cause found (lost-event race condition)
  • 14 Jul 13:13-13:38: partner given scaling explanation + 9 affected IDs
  • 14 Jul 13:19-13:54: engineers privately conclude scaling fix doesn't work, partner never told
  • 14 Jul 15:05: last human message, 'TBC tomorrow'
  • 15 Jul 08:40 CEST: still no further activity

→ Correct the record with the partner now that the scaling fix is known not to work, and confirm/credit the 3 newly-reported player IDs.

INC-13176 slack intercomP2open
[P2] Intercom Incident : Campeon Nexto Production

C10/D15: fix has been deployed and partner-confirmed with the Intercom ticket closed for a day now, yet incident.io still shows 'Investigating' with no change, and a second untracked bug still has no follow-up.

AI-agent Bedrock tool-name validation error bricked a Campeon Nexto partner's conversation thread. Fix merged/deployed by 14 Jul, partner confirmed working, Intercom ticket closed - but incident.io still shows 'Investigating' with no change since last read. A second bug (get_segments context overflow) surfaced, worked around manually, no follow-up tracked.

Open breaches

  • D15: second bug and its manual workaround entirely absent from incident.io record
Proposed / pending
  • sanitize Bedrock tool-call names - implemented, PR #459
  • fix get_segments context overflow - manual workaround only, no follow-up ticket
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-10T09:26
  • fix deployed 14 Jul - confirmed working
Timeline (4)

  • 13 Jul: PR merged
  • 14 Jul: deployed, partner confirms fix works, ticket closed; second bug found and worked around
  • 15 Jul 08:40 CEST: no new activity; incident.io status still 'Investigating', unchanged since 14 Jul 09:41 UTC

→ Update incident.io to Resolved/Closed to match the confirmed fix, and open a separate follow-up ticket for the get_segments overflow bug so it isn't lost.

INC-12617 slackP2open
[FIRING:3] Fusion API errors Alerting

C9/C10/B5/A4: partner informed once 07 Jul and never re-chased in 8+ days; alert auto-resolved 5+ days ago with no formal closure; same 401 pattern has recurred as INC-13895.

Fusion API 401 errors; root cause ruled out as FT-side, partner informed via Teams DM 07 Jul and never re-chased since. Bot flagged silent-close risk twice; manual escalation produced no response. Confirmed the exact 401 pattern has recurred only once more, as INC-13895 (14 Jul) - no 3rd occurrence yet.

Open breaches
  • C9: no partner re-chase in 8+ days
  • C10: alert cleared 5+ days ago, never formally closed
  • B5: silent-close warnings and manual escalation ignored
  • A4: recurring Fusion API 401 pattern (2nd occurrence INC-13895), not escalated to owning team
Proposed / pending

  • inform partner - done once, never re-chased
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-07T14:19
  • last partner message unanswered
Timeline (5)

  • 07 Jul 15:47: status -> Waiting on Partner
  • 09-10 Jul: bot nudges + manual escalation, no response
  • 13 Jul: underlying alert auto-resolves, no closure comms
  • 15 Jul: still 'Waiting on Partner', no new activity; recurrence confirmed as INC-13895 only

→ Have the lead re-chase the partner directly, formally close the incident since the underlying alert cleared days ago, and escalate the recurring Fusion API 401 pattern to the owning team before a 3rd occurrence.

INC-13757 slackP2open
Campeon: Incorrect games mappings

D15/B8 persist: incident.io still shows Daniel Lindengren as Lead and stale status despite the vacation handoff, but Samuela is now visibly close to a working fix (recommitted endpoint, about to run non-dry-run) — real progress, just not reflected in the record.

Incorrect game mappings for Campeon escalated brand-wide (~67.5k rows); confirmed crm-api resolver bug also hits gembet/wsm-casino; PRs #2509/#2510 remain unmerged, and REW-188's candidate fix was confirmed NOT to solve the issue. Samuela Lundborg is now actively re-working the reconciliation endpoint fix — hit a timeout, recommitted with an in-memory games/aliases lookup to avoid per-entry queries, and is ready to run the non-dry-run mode. incident.io still lists Daniel Lindengren (on vacation) as Incident Lead and status/summary remain unchanged since creation.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io not refreshed since creation despite scope escalating to brand-wide/multi-brand and active fix work ongoing
  • B8: incident.io still lists Daniel Lindengren (on vacation) as Incident Lead despite the Slack handoff to Sam Lundborg
  • unresolved time-sensitive customer impact: stuck player's challenge nearing expiry with no confirmed retro-credit
Proposed / pending
  • merge PR #2509 (deterministic resolver) - still open
  • merge PR #2510 (reconcile endpoint) - still open
  • REW-188 backoffice PR - confirmed NOT to fix the issue, no replacement fix proposed yet
  • manual retro-credit for stuck player - flagged, still not actioned
  • dry run of the recon endpoint - in progress by Samuela, result not yet known
Timeline (7)
  • 13 Jul: incident created (one player), scope found brand-wide same evening
  • 14 Jul: crm-api resolver bug confirmed, PRs #2509/#2510 opened, unmerged
  • 15 Jul 08:21-08:38 CEST: Daniel hands off to Sam Lundborg via Slack, opens REW-188
  • 15 Jul 08:21-08:44 CEST: Linus tests REW-188 PR live, confirms it does not solve the issue
  • 15 Jul 08:40 UTC check: incident.io Lead/status/summary all unchanged since creation
  • 15 Jul 10:57-11:29 CEST: Samuela gets Campeon Nexto to latest stable, shares the crm-api diff, and begins dry-running the reconciliation endpoint; bot checks in mid-run
  • 14:35 CEST: Samuela recommits reconciliation-endpoint fix (in-memory game/alias lookup to fix timeout), ready for non-dry-run

→ Formally reassign Incident Lead to Sam Lundborg in incident.io, post a status update reflecting brand-wide scope and the failed REW-188 fix, and confirm the stuck player's retro-credit before challenge expiry.

segurobet: no consumers on queue segurobet.consentsmanager

Partner admitted 9 Jul they're 'at a loss', no ETA ever given. 3rd segurobet/BetConstruct reliability incident in ~5 weeks, never escalated to account management. No new activity since 10 Jul.

  • 01-06 Jul: repeated chases, non-committal replies
  • 09 Jul: partner admits 'at a loss'
  • 10 Jul: last substantive chase
  • 15 Jul: no new activity since 10 Jul - 6+ days total silence, 18+ days total duration
  • 15 Jul 12:46 CEST: duplicate alert (INC-14128) auto-merged in, no human commentary; 15 Jul check: no new substantive activity

INC-14098 slackP2open 6h
Shuffle - Intermittently getting blocked by CF whilst on VPN

C9/C11: over 3 hours after the CTO personally wrote and chased the partner comms, there is still no confirmation they were sent — the incident just changed hands to a new lead (James Hodgson) with no visible resolution of that open item.

Shuffle/Data-Studio CF-WAF false-positive blocking dashboards for internal + partner users. Patrik personally wrote the fix (merged) and drafted the partner-facing explanation at 11:11 CEST, then had to chase Joachim directly at 12:17 CEST over the 66-min delay in sending it. Since then: Lead formally handed from Joachim to James Hodgson (15:21 CEST), and the bot checked in again at 15:24 CEST asking whether it's time for an update — no confirmation anywhere that Patrik's tuned partner comms have actually gone out.

Open breaches
  • C9/C11: still no confirmation that Patrik's 11:11 CEST partner-facing draft has actually gone out, now 3h+ since he personally chased it
  • D15: no incident summary set
Proposed / pending
  • WAF rule exemption for dashboard GET requests - implemented, PR #15 merged
  • Partner comms draft by Patrik - written, tune-and-send still pending as of last read
Partner view
  • last partner message unanswered
  • Patrik's partner-facing draft, written 11:11 CEST - still not confirmed sent as of 12:17 CEST
Timeline (8)
  • 08:16:32Z incident created, Joachim Lead+Reporter
  • 08:25Z Gustav Tjäder + CTO Patrik Potocki pulled in, also hitting Supabets
  • 08:40Z Patrik posts root cause (OWASP CRS rule 949110) + fix proposal
  • 10:42-10:58 CEST Patrik builds and merges WAF rule PR #15 across all ft-crm.com
  • 11:11 CEST Patrik drafts full partner-facing explanation, tags Gustav/Joachim to send it
  • 12:16-12:17 CEST Patrik chases Joachim directly ('did you communicate or Joachim?'), CCs Andrea Azzopardi, warns against just copy-pasting; Joachim: 'got a bit busy... will inform them'
  • 15:21 CEST Lead handed from Joachim Farrugia to James Hodgson
  • 15:24 CEST bot check-in nudge for an update — still no confirmation partner comms sent

→ Get explicit confirmation from James Hodgson that the tuned Shuffle/Supabets partner comms have gone out; if not, this is now a multi-hour-overdue CTO-flagged item.

🟡 Watch 15

INC-11707 slack intercomP1open
[P1] Intercom Incident : Betpipo Production

Previous C10/D15 breaches (unanswered partner question, stuck 'Investigating') are now resolved: root cause found, all 4 fix PRs merged and deployed, and the partner was proactively informed today — a clean recovery, now just watching for partner confirmation.

Betpipo partner reported repeated logouts from 26 Jun; root cause confirmed as a shared UserTokens table collision (player logins invalidating admin sessions, not scoped by IsAdminToken). All 4 fix PRs (91, 2502, 2503, 5302) merged and a custom release deployed to Betpipo/Betoffice at 14:51 CEST. Matteo informed the partner via Intercom at 14:33 UTC with the root cause and fix explanation, closing the previous multi-day silence. Plan: close in ~2 days if no further reports.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io status just moved to 'Waiting on Partner' - reasonable, not yet a breach; watch for partner reply over the next 2 days
Proposed / pending
  • scope token invalidation to IsAdminToken (4 PRs) - all merged and deployed 15 Jul
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-26T09:08
  • multiple 'stabilized' claims (01,03,06 Jul) all contradicted by recurrence
Timeline (9)
  • 01 Jul: first fix declared to stabilize, closed same day
  • 03/06 Jul: reopened, recurring
  • 10-13 Jul: real root cause found (UserTokens collision), 4 PRs approved
  • 15 Jul 06:16 UTC: 2 of 4 PRs merged (diagnostics only)
  • 15 Jul 07:24 UTC: internal note asks partner to confirm status - not customer-facing
  • 15 Jul (this check): no further activity; status still 'Investigating' since 06 Jul
  • 13:39 CEST: all 4 PRs merged (fusion-api #91, crm-api #2502/#2503, backoffice-v2 #5302)
  • 14:51 CEST: custom release deployed to Betpipo/Betoffice (platform release 19391)
  • 14:33 UTC: Matteo posts root-cause + fix explanation directly to the partner via Intercom, ending the prior 5-day silence

→ Post an actual customer-facing reply to the partner's 10 Jul question, and get a firm date for merging/deploying the remaining IsAdminToken-scoping PRs.

private — review directly

INC-9820 slackP2open
RDS OOM all brands - gradually increasing overtime

A4: a single minor gap remains - the activity-manager-go resilience follow-up has no owner - while the other two follow-ups are already remediated/tracked.

Fleet-wide RDS Performance Schema memory leak, fixed via PR #416 (merged 1 Jul), rolled out fleet-wide by 9 Jul. On 14 Jul the lead closed remaining gaps: capping follow-up marked completed, new RAM/swap-alarm follow-up created and assigned. Only one follow-up (activity-manager-go resilience) remains outstanding and unassigned.

Open breaches
  • A4: one follow-up (activity-manager-go resilience) remains outstanding and unassigned as closure nears
Proposed / pending
  • Cap Performance Schema memory (PR #416) - done, follow-up marked completed 14 Jul
  • RAM/swap alarm - now a tracked, assigned follow-up as of 14 Jul
  • activity-manager-go resilience fix - still outstanding, unassigned
Timeline (6)
  • 16 Jun: created, escalated P2
  • 01 Jul: PR #416 merged
  • 06-09 Jul: fleet-wide staged rollout
  • 14 Jul 13:35 UTC: capping follow-up marked completed
  • 14 Jul 16:01 UTC: new RAM/swap alarm follow-up created and assigned to Simon Brown/SRE
  • 15 Jul: no change since
INC-14039 slackP2open
Maxima: no consumers on queue maxima.singularity.clickhousewriter

C10: PVC resize still hasn't landed ~15h after creation, but root cause is now fully diagnosed with senior engineering (incl. Patrik) engaged and Matteo actively applying the known fix — good visible progress, not a silent drift.

Maxima ClickHouse-writer PVC resize got stuck: Maxima's volume is a legacy in-tree AWS EBS volume (not CSI-native like every other brand), and a stale/orphaned NodeResizePending marker silently swallowed all three resize attempts (1600/1700/2607Gi) with zero AWS calls actually made. Root cause fully diagnosed by Joachim; Patrik Potocki, Agustín, Andrew, Curt Healey and Max Holdaway all joined the channel around 14:06 CEST. Matteo Rapisarda has now taken it on directly, confirming this matches a PVC-upgrade-stuck pattern he's fixed before.

Open breaches
  • C10/D15: incident.io still shows 'Fixing'/'Investigating' churn; PVC still not actually resized after ~15h, though root cause is now fully understood and Matteo is actively fixing
Proposed / pending
  • Increase/persist ClickHouse PVC size - applied live; persistence in IaC/config not yet confirmed
Timeline (10)
  • 01:29:42Z created, auto-declined 15min later, recommendation unactioned
  • 05:31:44Z Joachim joins, monitoring
  • 06:26-06:27Z reopened
  • 06:32-06:33Z PVC upgraded 1380Gi->1600Gi
  • 06:49:43Z remaining alert resolved
  • 08:40 CEST 15 Jul: incident.io status still 'Fixing', not closed ~2h since resolution
  • 11:46 CEST Joachim Farrugia takes over as Lead from Gérard E. Pelayo; INC-14080 merged in
  • 11:47 CEST Joachim identifies the PVC resize never issued ControllerExpandVolume to AWS - resizer likely wedged after the rapid double edit
  • 14:04-14:06 CEST Joachim posts full root-cause diagnosis (legacy EBS vs CSI-native), pulls in Patrik/Agustín/Andrew/Curt/Max
  • 16:40-16:41 CEST Matteo Rapisarda joins, recognizes this as a repeat of a PVC-upgrade-stuck issue he's fixed before, takes it on
INC-13917 slack intercomP2open
Campeon: Slow processing on credit service (from lifecycles)

D15/C11: incident.io remains completely unrefreshed despite a completed infra mitigation and partner comms having gone out; the partner-facing explanation is still the generic 'performance bottleneck' rather than the internally-confirmed root cause — but the mitigation is done and the partner has been kept informed, so this is a watch item, not an active breach.

Campeon lifecycle coin-crediting delayed up to 2h on 11 Jul, root cause auto-claim + serialized credit path. Overnight mitigation applied and lead formally transferred to Sam Lundborg. Since the last check: the team (Matteo/Samuela/Linus) debated and completed an 8xlarge instance upgrade as a short-term mitigation (finished ~13:35 CEST), and Linus proactively informed the partner via Intercom that infrastructure was being upgraded, then confirmed completion. Partner sent one more delay example just before the fix landed.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io still has zero status updates despite the completed mitigation, lead handoff and partner comms — record not reflecting reality
  • C11: partner told a generic 'performance bottleneck', internally-confirmed root cause (auto-claim/serialized credit path) still not disclosed
Proposed / pending
  • Temporary: scale credit-service compute/replicas to 8xlarge - completed 15 Jul ~13:35 CEST
  • Permanent: credit VC directly vs auto-claim bonus - undecided
  • Add audit trail for Rewards bonus-config edits - raised, not tracked
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-14T11:18
  • Rewards team confirmed a performance-bottleneck backlog - partial/generic explanation
Timeline (9)
  • 11 Jul: 82k-message campaign takes ~1h50m to drain vs usual 15-30min
  • 14 Jul 11:18 UTC: incident created P2, Partner Manager requests P1 treatment internally
  • 14 Jul: root cause traced to auto-claim flag + serialized credit path
  • 15 Jul 08:08-08:54 CEST: Daniel hands off to Sam Lundborg before vacation
  • 15 Jul 09:45 CEST: Matteo applies temporary scaling mitigation, reassigns Lead to Sam Lundborg
  • 15 Jul 08:09 UTC: bot nudges for a status update - still unanswered
  • 15 Jul 07:50 UTC: Matteo sends partner Intercom follow-up (81k backlog, apology) - omits mitigation/lead change
  • 15:12-15:35 CEST team confirms and completes RDS instance upgrade to 8xlarge as temporary mitigation
  • 15:00-15:02 UTC (13:00-13:02) Linus informs partner via Intercom of the infra upgrade, then confirms it's complete

→ Post the incident.io update the bot is prompting for (mitigation applied, Sam Lundborg now lead), and reconcile the partner-facing explanation with the internal RCA and disclosed mitigation status.

INC-13895 slackP2open
[FIRING:1] Fusion API errors Alerting (spinbet-terragon: 401)

B5/A4 partial remediation: the re-chase that was needed has now gone out to the partner, closing the main C9 gap; the bot's silent-close nudge is still technically unanswered and the recurring pattern (2nd in a week) still isn't tracked to an owning team, so this stays a watch item rather than a closed loop.

Fusion API 401 errors blocked on-site notifications/Rich Inbox for Spinbet Terragon. Same alert family as INC-12617 recurring a week later. Partner was proactively notified once ~4h9m after alert fired but had gone unanswered for over a day; Joachim re-chased the partner again this morning (15 Jul) asking them to check on their end. Still awaiting a reply; bot's earlier silent-close nudge remains formally unanswered.

Open breaches
  • B5: bot's silent-close nudge from 14 Jul still formally unanswered, though a re-chase to the partner did go out this morning
  • A4: 2nd Fusion API 401 incident in a week (see INC-12617), not yet escalated to the owning team
Proposed / pending
  • identify partner-side root cause (end-user auth) - flagged, not confirmed or communicated
Partner view
  • last partner message unanswered
  • will update when more information available - still no follow-up sent
Timeline (8)
  • 14 Jul 08:24 UTC alert fires
  • 14 Jul 09:22 -> Investigating, 10:24 lead accepted
  • 14 Jul 12:33 UTC partner informed, status -> Waiting on Partner
  • 14 Jul 13:12 UTC Nazareno takes over as lead
  • 14 Jul 18:22 UTC bot nudge unanswered
  • 15 Jul 08:40 CEST: still no further activity, status unchanged
  • 15 Jul (this morning): Joachim re-chases partner again on the intermittent 401 errors
  • 15:15 CEST: bot check-in nudge, no reply yet

→ Re-chase the partner for the API-exchange info, now well over 24h since the ask, and respond to the unanswered bot nudge instead of letting it silently close.

INC-10045 slack intercomP2open
[P2] Intercom Incident : Power Play Production

C10: partner relationship is now in good shape (engaged, agreed to interim mitigation), but the update_epoch permanent fix still has no tracked follow-up with an owner/date — the one remaining gap.

GiG (Power Play's upstream KYC) event-ordering bug leaves stale block tags, blocking new-player journeys. Matteo sent the partner (Anna Koller) a detailed investigation writeup; she replied positively and agreed to a weekly manual re-sync as an interim fix while FT evaluates the update_epoch ordering-key fix long-term. Partner explicitly confirmed GiG never actually shipped a fix on their end (Option 1 dead). Good partner engagement, but the update_epoch fix still has zero incident.io/follow-up tracking.

Open breaches
  • C10: update_epoch fix recommended twice, still zero tracked incident.io follow-up despite partner now actively engaged and waiting on it
Proposed / pending
  • historical re-sync - done multiple rounds
  • GiG source-side tag fix - partial, ONT/119 still pending
  • update_epoch as ordering key - proposed twice, zero incident.io follow-ups tracking it
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-16T12:16
  • notify once re-syncs complete - kept
  • evaluate update_epoch reordering fix - in progress, no ticket yet
Timeline (7)
  • 18 Jun: root cause validated same day, 722 users affected
  • 06 Jul: closed against partner's explicit stay-open request
  • 10 Jul: partner reopens with new ONT/119 issue
  • 13 Jul: still ~8 new stuck accounts/week, update_epoch fix proposed again
  • 14 Jul 06:54 UTC: status moved back to 'Waiting on Partner'; zero tracked follow-ups confirmed
  • 15 Jul: no change
  • 14:35-14:38 UTC: partner (Anna Koller) replies positively, agrees to weekly manual re-sync interim, confirms GiG never fixed their side

→ Create a tracked incident.io follow-up for the update_epoch fix with an owner and due date now that the partner is expecting it.

INC-12179 slackP3open
failed to resolve container IP after 3 attempts

A4/D15: recurring failure class (3rd occurrence) with RCA and 4 ranked follow-ups filed 04 Jul; follow_up_list confirms all 4 still 'outstanding' 11 days later and status is still stuck 'Investigating' with an unanswered bot nudge.

llm-agent tool execution failing on Daytona sandbox cold-resume. Root cause confirmed 04 Jul with high confidence; 4 ranked follow-ups filed same day. follow_up_list re-checked directly: all 4 fixes remain 'outstanding' 11 days later; bot resolve-nudge unanswered; no new activity.

Open breaches
  • A4: 3rd occurrence of this recurring failure class - all 4 follow-ups confirmed still outstanding 11 days after RCA
  • D14/D15: status stuck 'Investigating' since 04 Jul, bot resolve-nudge unanswered 8+ days
Proposed / pending
  • probe toolbox data-plane (real fix) - outstanding
  • raise DAYTONA_EXEC_MAX_ATTEMPTS (band-aid) - outstanding
  • user-facing transient-error copy - outstanding
  • reduce cold-resume churn - outstanding

  • 04 Jul RCA posted + 4 follow-ups filed
  • 07 Jul bot nudge unanswered
  • 15 Jul: follow_up_list re-confirms all 4 follow-ups still 'outstanding', status unchanged since 04 Jul
INC-10810 slackP3open
12play brands fire late

D15 persists (incident.io Lead/status stale), but active partner-facing maintenance coordination is now underway and well-organized - a positive development worth tracking to completion.

12play/we88 (Momentum) players received rewards 45-50min late due to RDS overload, a known daily 4pm choke pattern that finally got a real fix (4 PRs, 2 DB upgrades over 3 weeks). Daniel Lindengren's 15 Jul handover to Sam Lundborg (RabbitMQ-upgrade coordination/cutover) was a properly executed handover in Slack, but incident.io still lists Daniel (on leave until 10 Aug) as Incident Lead and status is unchanged at 'Fixing' since 10 Jul. No new activity since last check.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io status stuck 'Fixing' since 10 Jul and Incident Lead field still shows Daniel Lindengren (now on leave) - the real handover happened in Slack but hasn't been reflected in the record
Proposed / pending
  • ClickHouse migration for active-challenges query - done
  • Content-variable reload-storm fix (PR #322) - merged
  • Rewards query split (PR #1914) - merged
  • crm-api caching (PR #2471) - merged
  • RDS instance upgrade - done
  • RabbitMQ 4.x + instance upgrade - date being coordinated with partner, cutover assigned to Sam Lundborg
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-24T16:00
  • update 12play via Intercom on fix progress - discussed internally, completion not confirmed

  • 25 Jun: incident created; daily 4pm RDS choke flagged as previously known
  • 30 Jun-9 Jul: PR #322, #1914, #2471 merged; DB instances upgraded
  • 10 Jul: Lead handed to Daniel Lindengren
  • 15 Jul 08:49 CEST: Daniel hands off to Sam Lundborg with a specific, actioned brief
  • 15 Jul: incident.io record confirmed still unchanged - Lead field still Daniel Lindengren (now on leave), status still 'Fixing'
  • 15 Jul 11:18-11:27 CEST: Linus loops in Agustín/Andrew/Samuela to schedule the RDS/cluster upgrade maintenance window with the partner (next Wednesday, ~1am FT time); scope covers the whole Momentum cluster (onetwoplay, onetwoplaysg, onetwoplayth) plus staging, timed around the 22 Jul cutoff
INC-13664 slack intercomP3open
[P3] Intercom Incident : SBTSolutions Production

D15/C10: incident.io still shows Waiting on Partner and no resolution message has reached the partner even though Matteo considers it fixed - echoing the earlier C11 breach where the partner was told a change was pending while already applied without sign-off.

SBTSolutions reported delayed bonus/activity-trigger processing; Matteo migrated the messaging system to Kafka without partner sign-off, then told the partner it would be scheduled - misrepresenting work already done. New: Matteo said in Slack 'this can be pretty much closed', but incident.io is still 'Waiting on Partner' and no resolution message has gone to the partner confirming the fix.

Open breaches
  • D15: stuck 'Waiting on Partner' since 13 Jul, not updated despite believing it's fixed
  • C11: partner told migration would be scheduled when already silently applied
  • C10: no resolution/closing message sent to partner despite the fix being considered done
Proposed / pending
  • Migrate to Kafka (root fix) - implemented 14 Jul, considered effective 15 Jul, still not formally confirmed resolved with partner or in incident.io
Partner view

  • Kafka migration during low-traffic window, no partner action needed - told after already applied; fix appears to be holding, not yet confirmed to partner

[P3] Intercom Incident : Slott Production [deleted]

D15/B5 unchanged: incident.io remains stale despite the Intercom side closing out 14 Jul, and the original escalation to Jeremy lapsed unacknowledged before being picked up via workflow.

Slott partner asked FT AI chat for a downloadable dataset; a widget bug truncated it. Fábio Silva manually delivered the data via Intercom; AI Team confirmed root cause and closed the Intercom ticket 14 Jul. incident.io still reads 'Investigating', untouched since 13 Jul - no update reflects the 14 Jul Intercom closure.

INC-12754 slackP3open
Campeon Nexto unable to login

D15/C10: fix delivered and confirmed working same day (08 Jul) but the incident.io record still shows 'Fixing' P3 status a full week later, with an unanswered bot resolve-nudge.

Campeon Nexto users couldn't log in due to a v2/v3 URL-version mismatch. Root cause found and workaround delivered within ~15 minutes; Patrik personally cleared Cloudflare cache and restarted crm-api same day (08 Jul), fix held. incident.io has now sat in 'Fixing' P3 status for a full week with no new activity.

Open breaches
  • D15/C10: incident.io stuck in 'Fixing' P3 status 7+ days after the fix was confirmed working same day; bot nudge unanswered
Proposed / pending
  • clean URL/different browser workaround - delivered, effective same day
  • Cloudflare cache clear + crm-api restart - done 08 Jul
  • core-xp escalation floated - not clearly followed up

  • 08 Jul 14:25 incident created (P4)
  • 08 Jul 14:39-15:31: root cause found, workaround given, Patrik resolves same day
  • 10 Jul 14:17: severity raised P4->P3
  • 11 Jul 00:33: bot nudge unanswered
  • 15 Jul: no new activity; status still 'Fixing', unchanged since 10 Jul
INC-10902 slackP4open
H2Bet incidents - night - DO NOT CLOSE

A4/D15: producer/consumer Status-field type mismatch identified 26 Jun remains unfixed 19 days later (2 more alerts merged in as late as 12 Jul); the 13 Jul 'close if stable' checkpoint has passed by 2 days with no update. Watch-level given P4 with partial remediation already shipped.

Recurring H2Bet nightly ClickHouse-writer queue pileup, consolidated into this 'DO NOT CLOSE' bucket since 26 Jun. A separate log-volume config bug was fixed (PR #435), but the producer/consumer schema mismatch itself is still unfixed. The promised 'close Monday 13 Jul if stable' checkpoint has now passed by 2 days with no update logged; no new activity since last check.

Open breaches
  • A4: root-cause payload type-mismatch bug identified 26 Jun, still unfixed ~19 days later; 2 more instances merged in as late as 12 Jul
  • D15: incident.io stale - no update since 09 Jul, promised 13 Jul checkpoint passed by 2 days unaddressed
Proposed / pending
  • Fix Status field schema/type mismatch end-to-end - still not implemented
  • ClickHouse log-volume config fix - done, PR #435 merged 9 Jul
  • Define sustainable autoscaling plan - no evidence of implementation
  • Restart/monitor, close if stable by 13 Jul - checkpoint passed without action
Timeline (5)
  • 26 Jun: grouped under this bucket, alerts attached
  • 27 Jun: pileup recurs, RDS scaled, Status-field unmarshal error confirmed
  • 9 Jul: log-volume bug fixed (PR #435 merged); plan to close 13 Jul if stable
  • 12 Jul: 2 more matching alerts merged in
  • 15 Jul: still 'Monitoring' status, no new activity; 13 Jul close-if-stable checkpoint remains unaddressed
INC-13889 slack intercomP4open
Calendars: Orphan (but live) door variations in the database(!)

A1/D15: no Incident Lead has ever been formally assigned in incident.io (only a Reporter role) even after a detailed Slack handover to Sam Lundborg; no summary set either. Low severity/impact (display bug, no player-facing effect) keeps this at watch level.

12Play's Chinese comms profile appeared missing from the backoffice - a display bug only, no player-facing impact. Rewards team found root cause and opened two PRs 14 Jul. Daniel Lindengren posted a detailed handover to Sam Lundborg on 15 Jul before vacation; no Incident Lead role has ever been formally assigned in incident.io (only Reporter). No new Slack or Intercom activity since last check - status and roles unchanged.

Open breaches
  • D15/A1: incident.io still has no summary set and no formally assigned Incident Lead (only Reporter role) despite a detailed Slack handover

  • backoffice PR - opened 14 Jul, pending test
  • rewards-backend PR - opened 14 Jul, pending test
  • handoff to Sam Lundborg to verify both PRs 15 Jul

  • fix release tomorrow/Thursday - not yet confirmed done

INC-10938 slackP4open 19d
Security incident: Malicious dashboard creation attempt

private — review directly

🟢 Under control 7

7 handled well: 2 new P2s resolved fast/clean today (14159, 14151), 1 P3 fully closed out proactively (13931 - G2, full payout audit), plus 4 low-risk new/metadata-only items (14158, 14153, 14152, 14078)

INC-14151 slack intercomP2open 83m
[P2] Intercom Incident : MeridianBet Production

MeridianBet couldn't add sports bonuses to a Peru onboarding flow (urgent launch request). Joachim Farrugia was paged first but the escalation expired unacknowledged; James Hodgson picked it up ~15s later via workflow. Matteo recognized it as the same issue as a prior incident (INC-10563) with an existing open FE PR, got it merged/deployed, and confirmed to the partner (14:41:59 UTC, ~1h16m after creation) that the fix is live.

Open breaches
  • B5 (minor, already covered): first-page escalation to Joachim Farrugia expired unacknowledged before James Hodgson picked it up automatically ~15s later — no actual gap in coverage
Proposed / pending
  • Merge existing FE fix (PR #5259/#5277) for bonus-validation bug - merged and deployed 15 Jul
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-15T11:58
  • fix deployed and confirmed to partner 14:41:59 UTC

  • 13:25:56Z James Hodgson acknowledges after Joachim Farrugia's escalation expired unacknowledged

  • 16:04-16:31 CEST PR merged, image built, deployed
  • 14:41:59 UTC Matteo confirms to partner (Jamie) that the fix is deployed
INC-14159 slackP2open 20m
[FIRING:1] ElastiCache Memory Utilized alert Alerting production-99-gen (true p2 aws-elasticache)

ElastiCache memory-utilization alert (production-99-gen, ~85% used). James Hodgson acknowledged within ~10 seconds of the page, was set as Incident Lead by workflow, accepted and moved straight to Monitoring within 8 minutes of creation. Clean, fast handling so far, no partner impact evident.

Timeline (3)
  • 14:29:18Z alert fires, incident created
  • 14:29:31Z James Hodgson acknowledges the page
  • 14:37:01-14:37:10Z accepted -> Investigating -> Monitoring, screenshot posted showing ~85.6% memory
INC-13931 slack intercomP3open
[P3] Intercom Incident : G2 Production

Handled well: thorough RCA, and Matteo proactively closed his own self-identified follow-up (checking other players affected by the event-lag window) by auditing all 193 payouts and sharing results with the partner unprompted.

G2 disputed a Welcome-Offer bonus for player 556. Matteo Rapisarda confirmed G2-side event-delivery lag as the true root cause (not a formula bug), then proactively audited all 193 Vegas Casino Welcome Offer payouts since launch against full entitlement history — 187/193 correct to the cent — and is sharing the full results with the partner unprompted. This directly actions the open item he'd flagged himself (checking whether other players were affected by the same event-lag window).

Proposed / pending
  • player adjustment for the $27.20 gap - pending partner alignment
  • full audit of all 193 Vegas Casino Welcome Offer payouts since launch - completed and shared with partner 15 Jul
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-13T09:49
  • Full root cause + remediation discussion sent 15 Jul 08:21 UTC; player-adjustment decision still pending partner alignment
Timeline (6)
  • 13 Jul 09:49 UTC partner reports via Intercom
  • 14 Jul: support escalates, NGR reconstructed, slots-only theory proposed (later superseded)
  • 15 Jul 07:53 UTC Matteo Rapisarda takes over as Incident Lead from Gérard
  • 15 Jul 08:11-08:21 UTC Matteo confirms G2-side event-delivery lag as true root cause, replies to partner, moves incident to Waiting on Partner
  • Flags G2's evening-wide event lag may have hit other players' NGR triggers that night - not yet actioned
  • 11:33 UTC: Matteo shares full 193-payout audit results with the partner, following up unprompted on his own earlier concern

Kafka lag on gamdom integration consumer (500k+ threshold). Took ~5.5h to be formally accepted (06:49->12:23) but is now in Monitoring with lead Giancarlo Laferla assigned; judged from incident.io metadata only this run (not selected for full deep-read: P3, has a lead, not yet 24h old).
